articles.title: Gold maintains the bearish bias as Trump's "final" deadline nears. What's next?
articles.expand: FUNDAMENTAL OVERVIEW Gold has been consolidating since Thursday’s selloff when Trump disappointed the market in his address to the nation by maintaining the hawkish stance towards Iran and reiterating the 2–3 week timeline for ending the war. We haven’t got any meaningful development in the meantime, although Trump set a “final” deadline for Iran t...

articles.title: Markets Today: Oil retreats, Gold rises as cautious tone dominates, Trump deadline now in focus
articles.expand: Oil prices jump due to escalating US-Iran conflict and a deadline for the Strait of Hormuz. European stocks are muted as caution reigns and Euro Area growth slows, showing stagflationary signals. The US Dollar is strongly bid ahead of key US CPI data, supported by Fed divergence

articles.title: UK: Energy shock weighs on outlook – Societe Generale
articles.expand: Societe Generale economists describe a quiet week in the UK, with Gilt yields easing as markets trimmed Bank of England (BoE) hike expectations after Governor Bailey pushed back on tightening.

articles.title: Yen-tervention likely to happen in the 161 to 163 range - Nomura
articles.expand: We already got a sense of that with the stronger verbal intervention last week, before some market optimism helped to keep further dollar strength in check. But as the volatility swings continue and the Middle East conflict drags on, the risks continue to stay heightened for the dollar to gain and for the yen to stumble even more. Nomura argues tha...

articles.title: Are Stagflation Fears Creeping Back Into the Picture? Here's What the Data and Fed Chair Jerome Powell Have to Say.
articles.expand: Key PointsThe Iran war is roiling Wall Street and triggering inflation fears among investors.

articles.title: EUR/CAD rises to near 1.6100 due to hawkish ECB tone
articles.expand: EUR/CAD gains ground as the Euro (EUR) receives support from the hawkish tone of the European Central Bank (ECB). The currency cross is trading around 1.6100 during the European hours on Tuesday.

articles.title: NZD/USD: Tactical support eyed near 0.5630 – DBS
articles.expand: DBS Group Research’s Philip Wee expects the Reserve Bank of New Zealand to keep policy on hold at its April 8 meeting, looking through energy-driven inflation. He notes Governor Anna Breman’s conditional hawkishness if inflation expectations de-anchor.

articles.title: Eurozone Sentix Slumps as Recession Risk Returns and Inflation Fears Surge
articles.expand: Eurozone investor sentiment deteriorated sharply in April, with data signaling that the risk of another recession is coming back into focus just as inflation concerns surge again on elevated oil prices. Sentix Investor Confidence dropped from -3.1 to -19.2, well below expectations of -7.5, and marking the weakest reading since April 2025. The break...

articles.title: ECB: Focus on expectations as Oil shock bites – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Chief Economist Dr. Jörg Krämer argues that the European Central Bank should respond to the recent Oil price shock by prioritizing long-term inflation expectations. He notes that higher energy costs are both lifting inflation and weighing on growth, creating a policy dilemma.

articles.title: USD/CAD Price Forecast: Bulls seem hesitant ahead of Trump’s Hormuz deal deadline
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair attracts some dip-buyers near the 1.3900 mark on Tuesday and reverses part of the previous day's slide as fading hopes for a US-Iran ceasefire revive demand for the safe-haven US Dollar (USD).

articles.title: US: Fed data watch shapes rate outlook – BBH
articles.expand: Brown Brothers Harriman’s (BBH) Elias Haddad highlights a busy US data calendar for the Dollar, with February PCE, March CPI and the University of Michigan survey guiding inflation expectations.

articles.title: DXY: Bracing for deadline risk – ING
articles.expand: ING’s Chris Turner notes the Dollar (USD) remains supported as investors await a White House deadline linked to the US-Iran conflict and elevated energy prices. Strong US jobs data and resilient activity could see markets price Federal Reserve (Fed) hikes if Oil rises further.

articles.title: USD/JPY Price Forecast: Ascending 20-day EMA supports more upside
articles.expand: The USD/JPY pair trades calmly around 160.00 during the European trading session on Tuesday. The pair trades broadly sideways amid uncertainty surrounding the ongoing war in the Middle East.

articles.title: US Dollar Index holds near 100.00 on Iran war fears, Trump deadline
articles.expand: The US Dollar Index (DXY), which measures the value of the US Dollar (USD) against six major currencies, is holding ground after registering losses in the previous trading day and hovering around 100.00 during the European hours on Tuesday.

articles.title: Oil: War-driven supply risks and security fractures – Rabobank
articles.expand: Rabobank’s Senior Market Strategist Benjamin Picton highlights escalating geopolitical risks around Iran, the Strait of Hormuz and NATO fractures, with direct implications for Oil.

articles.title: CAD: A strong hedge against energy risks – BBH
articles.expand: Brown Brothers Harriman’s (BBH) Elias Haddad expects Canada’s March labor force survey to show a modest job rebound, while the Bank of Canada (BoC) can use its benign inflation backdrop to look through the Oil shock.

articles.expand: Prior 51.9 Well, don't let the headline estimate fool you. Spain's services sector increased solidly last month owing to a further rise in new business received by companies. However, new order growth softened to its lowest level in nine months as the latest Middle East developments start to give rise to uncertainty. Of note, new export sales decli...

articles.title: NZD/USD Price Forecast: Struggles above YTD low, around 0.5700 amid geopolitical risks
articles.expand: The NZD/USD pair struggles to capitalize on the previous day's modest gains and trades with a negative bias around the 0.5700 mark during the early European session on Tuesday.

articles.title: GBP/USD Price Forecast: Tests 1.3250 near nine-day EMA barrier
articles.expand: GBP/USD holds its position after registering over 0.25% gains in the previous day, trading around 1.3240 during the early European hours on Tuesday. The technical analysis of the daily chart indicates a persistent bearish bias, as the pair moves lower within the descending channel.

articles.title: AUD/USD: Capped rebound within broader range – UOB
articles.expand: United Overseas Bank’s (UOB) Quek Ser Leang and Lee Sue Ann note AUD/USD rebounded to 0.6938 instead of drifting lower, but the move lacked momentum. The pair is seen trading between 0.6890 and 0.6940 in the near term and within a wider 0.6835–0.6955 band over one to three weeks.

articles.title: Dollar steady as traders weigh escalating Iran war against ceasefire hopes
articles.expand: The euro was at $1.1542, while sterling last fetched $1.324. The dollar index, which measures the U.S. currency against six others, was last at 100. With many Asian and European markets closed on Monday, liquidity was thin.

articles.title: Easter miracles for Markets? – North American Session Market Wrap for April 6
articles.expand: April 6th, 2026 North-American Session Recap – Trading are slowly returning to the action after a long week-end, with hopes still leading to some rebounds ahead of tomorrow's War deadline. Check up the latest trends and developments, daily asset and FX performance, what changed fundamentally and what's coming up in the session ahead.

articles.title: USD/CHF Price Forecast: Rejected at 0.8000 as double top looms
articles.expand: USD/CHF failed to clear key resistance at 0.8000 on Monday, recoiling to the 0.7900 handle as a double-top chart pattern looms. At the time of writing, the pair trades at 0.7979, down 0.18%.

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Extra losses likely below 0.6830
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar continues to swing in line with the alternating risk appetite trends, all in response to a volatile geopolitical scenario in the Middle East.

articles.title: GBP/USD Weekly Forecast: Holds up, but cracks are starting to show
articles.expand: Another bearish week for the British Pound led to the second consecutive weekly retracement for GBP/USD, which was primarily driven by geopolitical concerns rather than domestic issues, while market participants currently do not expect the Bank of England (BoE) to resume interest rate cuts this year

articles.title: USD/JPY under pressure as markets price in a BoJ rate hike
articles.expand: The Bank of Japan’s communication is becoming a key driver for USD/JPY as markets increasingly price in a possible rate hike. Investors are watching whether the BoJ signals a broader tightening cycle, which could strengthen the yen and push the pair lower.
